title: hunteria ghanensis
intro: Hunteria ghanensis is a plant species native to West Africa, also known as the 'Yoruba bitter root', which belongs to the Apocynaceae family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which is used to treat various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and malaria. The health benefits of Hunteria ghanensis include its anti-inflammatory and antimalarial properties, which help to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms associated with malaria. In traditional herbal preparations, the bark of the Hunteria ghanensis plant is often dried and ground into a powder, which can be mixed with water to create a medicinal tea or used as an ingredient in various herbal remedies.
